claim: The explorer's claim is confirmed: YOLO demand evidence dispatch has boundary snapshot audit implemented in code with proper enforcement hooks, but the critical gap is that we have NO evidence that Claude Code actually respects allowedTools constraints at runtime. The boundary audit mechanism is well-designed but unverified, and there is a fundamental trust boundary where the harness must rely on Claude Code to enforce tool restrictions that we cannot observe or control from this code.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() implementation verified: uses git ls-files -co --exclude-standard, walks directory tree, builds SHA-256 file digests via createHash('sha256'), excludes allowed write roots
evidence.why: Confirms explorer's evidence claim about snapshot implementation

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() implementation verified: compares before/after Maps to detect deleted (missing from after), modified (digest changed), and added (new in after) files, returns sorted changes array
evidence.why: Confirms explorer's evidence claim about diff logic

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot called BEFORE agent loop with project root and allowed output directory exclusion
evidence.why: Confirms boundary protection timing is before execution

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot called AFTER agent loop and diffBoundarySnapshots compares snapshots to detect changes
Boundary violations are converted to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers with specific path and change type, prd_ready set to false when violations exist
evidence.why: Confirms enforcement mechanism blocks PRD readiness on mutations

evidence.summary: executionConfig() normalizes agent_tool_profile to 'research', 'full', or 'boundary' based on agentToolProfile value and allowFullAgentTools flag
evidence.why: Refines explorer's claim: normalization logic is conditional and depends on both profile string AND allowFullAgentTools boolean

evidence.summary: When provider is 'claude', executionConfig() sets claude_tools='', claude_allowed_tools='', claude_disallowed_tools='' (empty strings), disables slash commands, and disables session persistence
evidence.why: CRITICAL: Confirms that boundary settings are RESET to empty strings, not passed through from loaded config

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime() requires BOTH executeAgents=true AND allowAgentDispatch=true for actual agent execution; returns early without execution if either flag is false
DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION requires evidence records with scope field and specifies that project facts require project-scoped evidence with repo-relative paths
projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() checks if evidence has project scope without path locator, or if evidence is only external/unknown for existing-project facts, and returns blockers
evidence.why: Confirms validation that project facts need project evidence

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation() passes claude_allowed_tools and claude_disallowed_tools from config.ai to Claude CLI args via --allowedTools and --disallowedTools
evidence.why: Refines explorer's claim: provider-adapter DOES pass boundary settings to Claude, but only if they exist in config.ai

evidence.summary: gitFiles() uses git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z which returns tracked, committed, and staged files but does NOT include untracked files
evidence.why: CONFIRMS EXPLORER ASSUMPTION IS WRONG: git ls-files -co does NOT return complete project file set—it misses untracked files, which is a critical boundary detection gap

evidence.summary: walkFiles() falls back to directory traversal when gitFiles() fails, which DOES include untracked files
evidence.why: Confirms fallback logic mitigates git limitation somewhat

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() combines git files and walked files with [...new Set([...git, ...walkFiles(projectRoot)])]
evidence.why: Confirms union approach that includes both tracked and untracked files

assumptions: Claude Code CLI respects --allowedTools and --disallowedTools flags at runtime when passed as arguments
Claude Code does not have alternate execution paths that bypass tool allowlist enforcement
No concurrent file system modifications from other processes during agent execution window between boundary snapshots
SHA-256 digests are sufficient for detecting meaningful content changes (binary files, symlinks, etc.)
Agents cannot mutate project files and revert changes before boundary snapshot completes
The excludeDir list (.git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache) covers all directories that should not be in project boundary
Allowed write roots exclusion correctly permits artifacts output directory
Agents cannot mutate the .yolo directory structure outside the allowed output directory
Claude Code session persistence disable (--no-session-persistence) actually prevents cross-session state leakage
risks: CRITICAL: If Claude Code does not enforce allowedTools at runtime, full-tool agents could mutate project despite boundary policy, and boundary audit would only detect after the fact

CRITICAL: git ls-files -co --exclude-standard does NOT include untracked files by itself; boundary snapshot only catches untracked files because walkFiles() is also run, but this dependency is implicit
If agents can write to target project files during evidence gathering and revert before snapshot, boundary audit will not catch mutations
If concurrent processes modify project files during agent execution, false positive boundary violations would block PRD readiness incorrectly
If evidence agents cannot read files due to permissions but return assumptions as facts, project claims would be based on unverified data
If SHA-256 collides (extremely unlikely but theoretically possible), mutations could go undetected
If agents modify files in excluded directories (.git, node_modules, etc.), boundary snapshot would not detect these mutations
If agents spawn subprocesses that survive beyond the agent session and later modify files, boundary audit would not catch these delayed mutations
If the harness or config.ai is compromised before dispatch, the entire boundary protection mechanism could be bypassed by malicious configuration
missing: Actual test results showing boundary audit detecting and blocking project mutations from full-tool Claude agents
Test results showing Claude Code respecting --allowedTools and --disallowedTools flags under real agent execution with evidence tasks
Integration test results showing end-to-end evidence dispatch with boundary enforcement on actual demand sessions
Verification that provider-adapter invocation correctly passes boundary settings (claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools) to spawned Claude processes
Evidence that current dirty working tree state does not interfere with boundary snapshot logic
Test results showing agents CANNOT bypass allowedTools by using indirect execution paths (subprocesses, eval, dynamic imports, etc.)
Verification that walkFiles() permission failures do not silently drop files from boundary snapshot
Evidence that concurrent process modifications during agent execution are handled or detected

result.notes: Explorer evidence is accurate and code inspection confirms boundary audit implementation. However, CRITICAL TRUST BOUNDARY IDENTIFIED: This code can only detect mutations AFTER they occur. The actual prevention depends entirely on Claude Code respecting --allowedTools flags, which we cannot verify from code inspection alone. We are trusting an external process (Claude Code CLI) to enforce boundaries that this code can only observe, not control. The cross_check recommendation is upgraded to require VERIFIER to explicitly assess whether this trust boundary is acceptable for PRD readiness.
